name: Taj Mahal
history: The Taj Mahal was built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in 1632 in memory of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal. The construction took more than 20 years and involved thousands of artisans and craftsmen. The structure is made of white marble and is decorated with intricate carvings, precious stones, and calligraphy. It represents the peak of Mughal architecture and is recognized as one of the Seven Wonders of the World.

name: India Gate
history: India Gate was built in 1931 to honor the soldiers of the British Indian Army who died in World War I. Designed by Sir Edwin Lutyens, the monument stands tall as a symbol of bravery and sacrifice. Beneath it lies the Amar Jawan Jyoti, which honors unknown soldiers. Today it is one of the most visited landmarks in Delhi.

name: Red Fort
history: The Red Fort was built by Shah Jahan in 1648 when he moved his capital to Delhi. The fort served as the residence of Mughal emperors for nearly two centuries. Built with red sandstone, it showcases Mughal architecture with beautiful halls and gardens. Today it is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and hosts India's Independence Day celebrations.

name: Qutub Minar
history: Qutub Minar was constructed in 1193 by Qutb-ud-din Aibak. The tower is 73 meters tall and features intricate carvings and Quranic inscriptions. It marks the beginning of Muslim rule in India and is surrounded by several historical structures, forming a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Hawa Mahal
history: Hawa Mahal was built in 1799 by Maharaja Sawai Pratap Singh. The palace is known for its unique honeycomb design with 953 windows that allowed royal women to observe street life without being seen. It is an architectural marvel and one of Jaipur’s most iconic landmarks.

name: Amber Fort
history: Amber Fort was built in the late 16th century by Raja Man Singh. The fort is known for its artistic Hindu architecture and large ramparts, series of gates, and cobbled paths. Inside, it has beautiful palaces, halls, and temples decorated with intricate mirror work and carvings.

name: Mysore Palace
history: Mysore Palace is the official residence of the Wadiyar dynasty and is one of the most visited monuments in India. The palace was rebuilt in 1912 after a fire and showcases Indo-Saracenic architecture. It is famous for its grand Durbar Hall and dazzling illumination during festivals.

name: Golden Temple
history: The Golden Temple, also known as Harmandir Sahib, was founded by Guru Ram Das in the 16th century. It is the holiest site in Sikhism and is known for its gold-covered structure and peaceful surroundings. The temple serves free meals to thousands of visitors daily.

name: Charminar
history: Charminar was built in 1591 by Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah to mark the founding of Hyderabad. It features four grand arches and minarets and is surrounded by bustling markets. The monument is an important cultural and historical symbol of the city.

name: Gateway of India
history: The Gateway of India was built in 1924 to commemorate the visit of King George V and Queen Mary. Located on the waterfront in Mumbai, it became a symbolic entry point during British rule and later a departure point for British troops after independence.

name: Ajanta Caves
history: Ajanta Caves date back to the 2nd century BCE and are famous for their rock-cut architecture and ancient paintings depicting Buddhist stories. These caves were rediscovered in the 19th century and are now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Ellora Caves
history: Ellora Caves are a group of 34 rock-cut temples built between the 6th and 10th centuries. They represent three religions: Buddhism, Hinduism, and Jainism. The Kailasa Temple is the most famous structure, carved from a single rock.

name: Meenakshi Temple
history: Meenakshi Temple in Madurai is dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Shiva. It is famous for its towering gopurams covered with colorful sculptures. The temple is an important pilgrimage site and a masterpiece of Dravidian architecture.

name: Konark Sun Temple
history: Konark Sun Temple was built in the 13th century by King Narasimhadeva I. It is designed in the shape of a gigantic chariot with wheels and horses. The temple is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and an important example of ancient Indian architecture.

name: Lotus Temple
history: Lotus Temple was completed in 1986 and is known for its flower-like structure. It is open to all religions and promotes unity and peace. The temple is one of the most visited buildings in the world.

name: Sanchi Stupa
history: Sanchi Stupa was built by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE. It is one of the oldest stone structures in India and contains relics of Buddha. The site represents the early development of Buddhist architecture.

name: Khajuraho Temples
history: Khajuraho Temples were built between 950 and 1050 AD by the Chandela dynasty. They are famous for their intricate sculptures depicting everyday life, spirituality, and art. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Jaisalmer Fort
history: Jaisalmer Fort was built in 1156 by Rawal Jaisal. It is one of the largest living forts in the world where people still reside. The fort is made of yellow sandstone and is known as the Golden Fort.

name: Mehrangarh Fort
history: Mehrangarh Fort was built in 1459 by Rao Jodha. It stands 400 feet above the city of Jodhpur and is known for its massive walls and impressive architecture.

name: Ranthambore Fort
history: Ranthambore Fort is located inside the Ranthambore National Park and has a history dating back to the 10th century. It was an important strategic fort for Rajput rulers.

name: Victoria Memorial
history: Victoria Memorial was built in memory of Queen Victoria and completed in 1921. It is made of white marble and reflects Indo-British architecture.

name: Brihadeeswarar Temple
history: Built in 1010 AD by Raja Raja Chola I, this temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is known for its massive dome and granite structure.

name: Rameshwaram Temple
history: Rameshwaram Temple is one of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites. It is associated with Lord Rama and is known for its long corridors.

name: Jagannath Temple
history: Jagannath Temple in Puri is dedicated to Lord Jagannath. It is famous for the Rath Yatra festival and has great religious significance.

name: Tirupati Temple
history: Tirupati Temple is dedicated to Lord Venkateswara and is one of the richest temples in the world. Millions of devotees visit every year.

name: Dal Lake
history: Dal Lake in Srinagar is famous for its scenic beauty and houseboats. It is an important tourist attraction in Kashmir.

name: Nainital Lake
history: Nainital Lake is a natural freshwater lake surrounded by hills. It is a major tourist attraction in Uttarakhand.

name: Manali
history: Manali is a popular hill station in Himachal Pradesh known for its snow-covered mountains and adventure activities.

name: Goa Beaches
history: Goa beaches are famous for their scenic beauty, nightlife, and Portuguese heritage. It is one of the top tourist destinations in India.

name: Andaman Islands
history: The Andaman Islands are known for their crystal-clear waters, coral reefs, and rich marine life. It is a paradise for beach lovers and divers.
